title: We Are the Essentials assists PCSO in search for missing man from Frostproof
summary: We Are the Essentials is assisting the Polk County Sheriff's Office in a search for a missing man from Frostproof.

title: NASA chief visits Russia's space launchpad for U.S.-Russian crew launch
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman attended a scheduled launch Tuesday of a U.S.-Russian crew to the International Space Station.

title: Florida Rep. Buchanan speaks on daylight saving bill to be voted on in the House
summary: Florida Congressman Vern Buchanan said his bill to make Daylight Saving Time permanent year-round will be voted on in the House of Representatives on Tuesday.

title: Atlanta tightens security for England-Argentina World Cup semifinal
summary: Atlanta police are increasing security for Wednesday’s England-Argentina World Cup semifinal amid fan clashes and historic tensions.

title: Olive Garden brings back Never Ending Pasta Pass after 6-year hiatus
summary: Olive Garden is bringing back its Never Ending Pasta Pass for the first time in six years, offering 13 weeks of unlimited pasta for $100.

title: Pinellas County Schools getting public input on next round of changes, potential school closures
summary: The Pinellas County Schools is getting public input on next round of changes which could include potential school closures and consolidation to combat lower student enrollment and rising costs.

title: Trump drops planned Strait of Hormuz toll in favor of Gulf trade deals
summary: President Donald Trump reversed plans for a 20% Strait of Hormuz shipping fee, saying Gulf nations will instead pursue U.S. trade and investment deals.

title: Rare T. rex fossil sells for record $50.1 million at auction
summary: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton from South Dakota has become the world’s most expensive fossil sold at auction, after fetching a record $50,130,000 at Sotheby’s in New York Tuesday.

title: Inflation slowed in June as energy markets stabilized temporarily
summary: Inflation eased in June as lower gas and energy prices helped cool consumer costs, though overall inflation remains above the Federal Reserve’s target.

title: Earthquake aid keeps flowing from Florida to Venezuela, as volunteers unite to help recovery
summary: The 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes struck 39 seconds apart on June 24, killing at least 4,500 people with thousands more still missing.

title: An experimental Alzheimer's drug is showing some promise
summary: An experimental drug might help slow early Alzheimer's disease in a markedly different way than today's treatments — by lowering levels of a brain protein called tau, researchers reported Tuesday.

title: Trump administration opens endangered species’ habitats to development
summary: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act and eliminates a key protection for imperiled wildlife against logging, oil drilling and other activities.
